Weather¶

  • PySpace provides 2 methods that help you access data about Earth and Martian weather.

Earth Weather¶

Warning

To use this method, you need to specify the Weather API

1import pyspace
2
3nasa = pyspace.PySpace(weather_api_key="API_KEY")
earth_weather(self, location, ugroup="us", start_date='', end_date='', c_type="json"):

Returns data for the NASA APOD (Astronomy Picture of the Day).

Parameters

  • location (str (Name of country, city etc.)) –

  • ugroup (str) – The Unit Group

  • end_date (str) – Range of the weather data

  • c_type (str) – The content type in which data is returned.

Raises

  • TypeError – Raised if location, ugroup, start_date, end_date or c_type are not str

  • HTTPError – Raised if response fails. (When Status code is not 200)

Returns

Dictionary object with JSON data containing information about the weather of specified location, date range.

Return type

dict

Martian Weather¶

Attention

You do not need an API_KEY to retrieve data from the Insights (Mars Weather Data) API. Although it is highly recommended. Please read more information about NASA’s:ref:Rate Limits <rate_limits>

mars_weather(self, version=1.0):

Returns per Sol weather data for each of the last seven available Sols.

Parameters

version (int or float) – Version of the Insights (Mars Weather Data) API

Raises

  • TypeError – Raised if location, ugroup, start_date, end_date or c_type are not str

  • HTTPError – Raised if response fails. (When Status code is not 200)

Returns

list object containing dictionaries for each Sol followed by the average, min, and max temperature of that martian day (Sol) in Celcius

Return type

list[dict, …]

Note

We will soon add a unit parameter so that the user can choose the unit in which the temperatures are. (Fahrenheit or Celcius)
